task.title: Reconnecting AirPods to an iPhone
task.shortdesc: If your AirPods are not connecting automatically, you can manually reconnect them to your iPhone through the Bluetooth settings or by resetting the pairing.

ul.li: Your AirPods have sufficient charge. Place them in the charging case for at least 15 minutes if the battery is low.
Bluetooth is enabled on your iPhone. Go to Settings and confirm that Bluetooth is toggled on.
Your iPhone is running iOS 14 or later. Earlier versions may not support automatic switching and some pairing features.

context.p: AirPods occasionally lose their active connection after extended periods of inactivity, after being connected to a different device, or after a software update. This procedure walks you through re-establishing the connection manually.

step.cmd: Place both AirPods in the charging case and close the lid.
step.info: Wait 15 seconds before proceeding. This resets the AirPods' active connection state.
The Bluetooth screen shows all previously paired devices. Your AirPods should appear in the list under My Devices.

step.cmd: Open the AirPods charging case lid while holding the case close to your iPhone.
step.info: The AirPods status in the Bluetooth list should change from Not Connected to Connected within a few seconds.

step.cmd: If the AirPods do not connect automatically, tap the name of your AirPods in the Bluetooth list.
step.stepresult: Your iPhone initiates a manual connection request. The status changes to Connected when successful.

result.p: Your AirPods are now connected to your iPhone. Audio from your iPhone will route through the AirPods automatically. You will see the AirPods battery indicator in the iPhone status bar or in the Batteries widget.

postreq.p: If your AirPods still do not connect after following these steps, proceed to Resetting AirPods to factory settings to perform a full reset and re-pair from scratch.
